evidence_quotes.quote: Working dark patterns are a myth.
F2P is built on a small share of voluntary payers: even AppsFlyers 2024 gaming report notes that fewer than 5% of users make a purchase, so if tricks truly worked, wed see broad conversion lift.

paragraphs.text: Working dark patterns are a myth. F2P has in general very low conversion. F2P is built on a small share of voluntary payers: even AppsFlyers 2024 gaming report notes that fewer than 5% of users make a purchase, so if tricks truly worked, wed see broad conversion lift. Instead, such tactics tend to erode trust, drive refunds/chargebacks, and increase churn (see the FTCs Epic/Fortnite case). Please avoid a one-size-fits-all approach that would unintentionally disable responsible F2P design (bundles, multiple soft currencies, live-ops balancing). Industry voices warn that over-broad bans or misclassifying in-game currency could damage legitimate UX and burden smaller studios without improving outcomes; a principles-based path with clear guidance is preferable. Europes mobile-games sector is one of the continents clearest digital-economy success stories. The EU video-games industry is the fastest-growing cultural and creative sector, worth ~23.3bn in 2021 and still expanding, with tens of thousands of skilled jobs across the bloc. In 2024 mobile was the largest slice of Europes games revenue (44%) and ~90% of spend was digitalevidence of a competitive, exportable, born-digital industry Europe already leads in. European publishers are global champions: Supercell (Finland) continues to ship worldwide hits, Candy Crush still generates about $1B/year, and Voodoo (France) ranks among the worlds top publishers by downloads (8B+ lifetime). If new EU rules rely on case-by-case actions against individual developers, they risk an uneven playing field: non-EU studios with no EU establishment or representative can be hard to reach or slow to act, a gap the Commission itself flagged when calling for stronger cross-border CPC enforcement and more resources. Meanwhile, EU studios are fully reachable and will bear disproportionate fixed compliance costs, even when the worst offenders sit outside the Union. Europe finally leads in a consumer digital market. Lets not turn that into a cautionary tale. Precise, enforceable standards can curb abuse while allowing this success story to keep growing.
